name: Iolani Palace
tagline: The only royal palace on American soil — home to Hawaii's last monarchs, with electricity before the White House.
parking: Metered street parking; paid lots on King St
bestTime: Morning guided tours; Friday noon concerts by the Royal Hawaiian Band on the grounds (free)
talkStory: Home to Hawaii's last reigning monarchs — King Kalakaua and Queen Liliuokalani — Iolani Palace had electricity before the White House. Guided tours reveal the tragic story of Hawaii's overthrow, and the restored state rooms are magnificent. Locals view this as a deeply important cultural site, not just a museum. The grounds are beautiful and free to walk. Reserve guided tours in advance; Friday noon concerts by the Royal Hawaiian Band are a beloved tradition.
